title: Rust SCHC: MQTT-SN compress/decompress may panic on short UDP
description:
File: rust/lichen-schc/src/codec.rs
Line: 454

compress_mqtt_sn checks 'packet.len() < 40 + 8' but then accesses packet[40..42] for port extraction. If packet is exactly 48 bytes (header only, no payload), tail would be empty which is fine. But the u16::from_be_bytes calls could panic if the slice is wrong length - actually they use try_into which returns Result. However, the indexing packet[40], packet[41] is safe given the length check. No bug here on closer inspection.
close_reason: False positive - length check at line 435 ensures packet.len() >= 48 before accessing packet[40..48]. Indexing is safe.
